paper_title: Fe limitation decreases transcriptional regulation over the diel cycle in the model diatom Thalassiosira pseudonana

methods.description: Procedure for growing Thalassiosira pseudonana cultures under controlled conditions with varying iron and CO2/pH levels, and collecting samples for downstream analysis. This method describes the preparation of media, culture conditions, acclimation, growth monitoring, and sample collection.

steps.description: Axenic Thalassiosira pseudonana (CCMP 1335) was grown in batch cultures using chelexed Aquil media prepared according to [22] with the addition of either 1000 nmol L -1 or 50 nmol L -1 iron (EDTA at 100 μmol L -1 ).
steps.searchable_summary: prepare chelexed Aquil media with specific iron concentrations for algal culture

steps.description: All media and cultures were prepared in a NuAir laminar flow hood (model NU-S201-430, Plymouth, MN, USA) within a trace metal clean room.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare media and cultures in a trace metal clean room using a laminar flow hood

steps.description: Media and bottles were sterilized via microwave and the polycarbonate bottles used for cultures and experiments were washed with 10% hydrochloric acid and rinsed with MilliQ water.
steps.searchable_summary: sterilize media and wash culture bottles

steps.description: Cells were grown at 20˚C under a 12:12 light: dark cycle at 50 μmol photons m -2 sec -1 , a light level that limits the growth rate of T. pseudonana.
steps.searchable_summary: grow algal cultures under specific temperature, light cycle, and light intensity conditions

steps.description: Prior to start of the experiments, cells were grown at the same light level and acclimated to iron-replete (1000 nM) or iron-limiting (50 nM) conditions for at least a month (~20 generations at Fe-limiting,~40 generations at Fereplete), but not to their respective CO 2 /pH conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: acclimate algal cultures to iron-replete or iron-limiting conditions

steps.description: During this pre-experiment set-up, growth rates were determined by daily monitoring of chlorophyll a fluorescence using a 10-AU fluorometer (Turner Designs, Sunnyvale, CA, USA).
steps.searchable_summary: monitor algal growth rate daily using chlorophyll a fluorescence

steps.description: The starting cell concentration for the three experimental replicates was 30,000 cell/mL (determined by using a hemacytometer).
steps.searchable_summary: determine starting cell concentration using a hemacytometer

steps.description: We used two pCO 2 /pH conditions of 400 ppm/8.1 and 800 ppm/7.8, in combination with two Fe additions of 1000 nM and 50 nM. The resulting concentration of available Fe (hereafter referred as Fe') for each condition was calculated using equations from Morel and Herring [23]. We considered the four conditions as four different Fe' concentrations for the rest of the analysis. All experiments were performed in triplicate.
steps.searchable_summary: set up experimental conditions with varying pCO2/pH and iron levels and calculate available iron (Fe')
outputs: Experimental cultures under four conditions (varying Fe', CO2/pH)
Calculated Fe' concentrations for each condition

steps.description: All experimental samples were drawn from bottles via a syringe port 6 hours into the light (midday) and 6 hours into the dark phase (midnight).
steps.searchable_summary: collect experimental samples at specific time points (midday and midnight) using a syringe port

methods.method_name: Transcriptome Data Processing and Differential Expression Analysis
methods.description: Bioinformatic pipeline for processing raw Illumina sequence reads, quantifying gene expression, identifying differentially expressed genes, and performing pathway enrichment analysis, including KEGG analysis.

steps.description: Sequence reads from Illumina were trimmed with Trimmomatic 0.36 [27], run in paired-end mode with the following parameters: ILLUMINACLIP (cut adapter and other Illumina-specific sequences) was set to TruSeq3-PE.fa.2:30:10, Leading and Trailing thresholds were 25, the sliding window trimming approach was 4:25, the average quality level (AVGQUAL) was 30, and the minimum length (MINLEN) was 100.
steps.searchable_summary: trim Illumina paired-end sequence reads using Trimmomatic with specified parameters

steps.description: The reads were then mapped against the JGI Thaps3 FilteredModels2 genome (https://genome.jgi.doe.gov/Thaps3/Thaps3.home.html) using the sequence aligner Hisat2-2.1.0 [28] (maximum intron length: 2000, using rna-strandness option "RF" to account for the reverse complement nature of the reads, no discordant alignment).
steps.searchable_summary: map trimmed reads to the reference genome using Hisat2 with specified parameters

steps.description: Transcript abundances were quantified with HTSeq 0.6.1 using default settings, counting the number of fragments that aligned to a given gene in the JGI Thaps3 FilteredModels2 set.
steps.searchable_summary: quantify transcript abundances by counting aligned fragments per gene using HTSeq

steps.description: The edgeR pipeline was used to perform differential transcript abundance for each gene [29].
steps.searchable_summary: perform differential gene expression analysis using the edgeR pipeline

steps.description: We conducted pairwise comparisons between day samples and night samples at each Fe' concentration by using a generalized linear model (GLM) likelihood ratio test.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct pairwise differential expression comparisons between day and night samples using a GLM likelihood ratio test
outputs: Pairwise differential expression results (day vs night per Fe')

steps.description: Unless otherwise noted, all results shown in this work consider a significant differential expression at p<0.01 and FDR<0.05.
steps.searchable_summary: filter differential expression results for significance based on p-value and FDR thresholds
inputs: Differential expression results (with p-values and FDR)
outputs: List of significantly differentially expressed genes

steps.description: An enrichment analysis of genes that maintained differential expression between day and night was performed using DAVID (Database for Annotation, Visualization and Integrated Discovery) with the classification stringency set on "high".
steps.searchable_summary: perform functional enrichment analysis on differentially expressed genes using DAVID
inputs: List of significantly differentially expressed genes
outputs: Functional enrichment results (pathways, GO terms) from DAVID

steps.description: A pathway was considered enriched if p<0.05.
steps.searchable_summary: filter DAVID enrichment results for significance based on p-value threshold
inputs: Functional enrichment results from DAVID (with p-values)
outputs: List of significantly enriched pathways/terms from DAVID

steps.description: To perform a Kyoto Encyclopedia of Genes and Genomes (KEGG) pathway enrichment analysis, the JGI ID was mapped to the KEGG ID system using BlastKOALA (https://www.kegg.jp/blastkoala/).
steps.searchable_summary: map gene IDs (JGI) to KEGG IDs using BlastKOALA
outputs: Gene IDs mapped to KEGG IDs

steps.description: To determine the number of genes differentially expressed between the day and the night time points, we conducted a Fisher exact test for each of the 101 pathways with mapped JGI IDs and then performed a Benjamini Hochburg procedure to remove false positives (significant enrichment if the value was below 0.05).
steps.searchable_summary: perform KEGG pathway enrichment analysis using Fisher exact test and Benjamini Hochburg correction
inputs: Gene IDs mapped to KEGG IDs
List of differentially expressed genes (day vs night)
outputs: KEGG pathway enrichment results (Fisher exact test p-values, Benjamini Hochburg adjusted p-values)

steps.description: Selected KEGG pathways, as well as pathways for transcription factors [30] and signaling molecules [31] were further analyzed to look at differential expression over day: night within each pathway.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze differential expression patterns within selected KEGG, transcription factor, and signaling pathways
inputs: Gene lists for selected KEGG pathways
Gene lists for transcription factor pathways [30]
Gene lists for signaling molecule pathways [31]
outputs: Analysis results for differential expression within specific pathways

steps.description: Further gene curation of the KEGG pathways was done for photosynthesis, carbon fixation and glycolysis to produce complete pathways.
steps.searchable_summary: curate gene lists for specific KEGG pathways (photosynthesis, carbon fixation, glycolysis)
inputs: Initial gene lists for photosynthesis, carbon fixation, glycolysis KEGG pathways
outputs: Curated gene lists for photosynthesis, carbon fixation, glycolysis pathways

steps.description: Predictions for protein localizations in the carbon-related pathways was done following [32].
steps.searchable_summary: predict protein localizations for genes in carbon-related pathways

steps.description: At each Fe' level, we assessed the number of genes within a given pathway that were either differentially expressed between night and day (diel) or not differentially expressed (not diel), relative to the total number of genes outside a given pathway that were or were not diel.
steps.searchable_summary: assess the effect of Fe' level on diel expression enrichment within KEGG pathways
outputs: Assessment of diel enrichment per pathway at each Fe' level
